raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for {$420.00} plus tax using my American Express XXXX card. On first use, the mower smoked heavily and appeared defective. XXXX refused to accept a return, citing their policy against returns on gaspowered equipment once fuel has been added, even though adding gas is necessary to test the product. 

I pointed out that other major retailers, like XXXX XXXX and XXXX, accept returns on defective gaspowered equipment even if fuel was added. I contacted the manufacturer, but the first authorized service center did not handle XXXX products, and the next available shop was over XXXX miles away with a XXXX week wait. 

I filed a dispute with American Express. It was denied on XX/XX/year>, then again after supervisor review on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX Throughout the process, I provided a detailed timeline, email correspondence, and photo and video evidence showing the defect and my efforts to resolve the issue. I also asked to escalate to a higher department such as Executive Relations, but was told no such department exists. 

After the dispute was closed, I followed the exact steps XXXX and American Express were required for a refund to obtain documentation from a service center approving the return. The service center confirmed in writing that the mower was defective and unrepairable. I returned the mower to XXXX with that documentation. XXXX accepted the return and put in writing that I would receive a refund check in the mail within 3 weeks a few weeks later XXXX out of no where decided to go back on there agreement and only offer store credit dispute the fact I spent months doing all they asked of me and American express They even wrote a letter and signed a letter saying they will refund me by check with in XXXX weeks. 

American Express closed the dispute again, for reasons still unknown. This was despite the fact that the documentation clearly referred to the defective mower and was signed by the front end manager. As of XX/XX/year>, I have received no refund and the run around from American Express, even though both XXXX and Amex acknowledged the mower was returned. 

This is not a warranty issue it is a defective product, properly returned, dispite the extended lengths asked to process the refund, with a refund promised and not delivered. I am requesting CFPB assistance to ensure that American Express reopens the dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act and holds XXXX accountable for failing to honor their written agreement.
